Photons are elementary particles that represent quantized units of light and all other forms of electromagnetic radiation, traveling at the speed of light and carrying energy and momentum.

Photons are elementary particles that are the quantum of the electromagnetic field and mediate the electromagnetic force, essentially being the fundamental units of light and all other forms of electromagnetic radiation.

Photons are elementary particles that are the fundamental units of light and all other forms of electromagnetic radiation, characterized by their massless nature and ability to travel at the speed of light.

Photons are elementary particles that are the fundamental units of light and all other forms of electromagnetic radiation. They travel at the speed of light and carry energy and momentum, but have no mass or electric charge.
